canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, image description is consistently defined as the behavior of generating a textual account of an image's visual content. The prevailing usage frames this as describing entities, attributes, and their relationships, with one paper specifying that it involves "steps derived solely from visual information" ("Correcting Negative Bias in Large Language Models through Negative Attention Score Alignment"). A more specific framing distinguishes this from OCR, which is reserved for text-rich parts of an image. Some work extends the definition to encompass higher-level cognition, where models are expected to "understand and describe the story in the image through high-level cognitive reasoning" ("CharacterBox: Evaluating the Role-Playing Capabilities ofLLMs in Text-Based Virtual Worlds"). To operationalize this behavior, researchers use measurement instruments such as Q-Bench. The task is studied in relation to visual understanding, and its failure modes are frequently associated with hallucination and object hallucination.
papers: MIA-Bench: Towards Better Instruction Following Evaluation of Multimodal LLMs
Mitigating Object Hallucination in MLLMs via Data-augmented Phrase-level Alignment
TaskGalaxy: Scaling Multi-modal Instruction Fine-tuning with Tens of Thousands Vision Task Types
Towards Interpreting Visual Information Processing in Vision-Language Models
CharacterBox: Evaluating the Role-Playing Capabilities ofLLMs in Text-Based Virtual Worlds
Correcting Negative Bias in Large Language Models through Negative Attention Score Alignment
WebQuality: A Large-scale Multi-modal Web Page Quality Assessment Dataset with Multiple Scoring Dimensions

definitions.text: The observable behavior of generating a textual description of the content within an image.
definitions.paper: MIA-Bench: Towards Better Instruction Following Evaluation of Multimodal LLMs
